actions.description: Optimize energy efficiency by integrating all-electric heating and cooling systems, site-specific geothermal solutions, and district-scale thermal networks.
actions.funding: NYSERDA NYS Clean Heat Program, Federal Tax Credits (IRA), NYSERDA HVAC Programs
implementationProjects: Village Green Buildings & Parking Lots Upgrade Program
Village Clean Energy Zoning, Building Codes, and Permitting Process

actions.action: Climate Smart Siting and Building Design
actions.description: Integrate passive design, sustainable materials, and resource-efficient infrastructure to enhance energy efficiency and resilience in Sleepy Hollow's built environment.
actions.funding: NYSERDA New Construction Program, NYS Real Property Tax Exemption for Green Buildings, NYSEFC Green Resiliency Grant Program
implementationProjects: Village Green Buildings & Parking Lots Upgrade Program

actions.action: Embed Sustainability and Resiliency Standards in Village Infrastructure Projects
actions.description: Ensure all major infrastructure projects meet sustainability and climate resilience standards through LEED, Envision, and embodied carbon reduction strategies.
actions.funding: NYSDEC CSC Grant, NYSERDA Building Cleaner Communities Competition

actions.action: Adopt Energy Efficient Street, Traffic, and Open Space Fixtures
actions.description: Upgrade Sleepy Hollow's public lighting systems with LED technology and smart controls to reduce energy consumption, improve safety, and lower operational costs.
actions.funding: NYPA Smart Street Lighting NY Program
implementationProjects: Village Green Buildings & Parking Lots Upgrade Program

actions.action: Integrate Clean Energy Systems into Public and Private Buildings
actions.description: Expand renewable energy adoption, energy storage, and microgrids to enhance sustainability and resilience across Sleepy Hollow.
actions.funding: NY Green Bank, NY-Sun Initiative, NYSERDA Energy Storage Program, Federal ITC

actions.action: Expand and Enhance Municipal Aggregation for Renewable Energy
actions.description: Leverage Community Choice Aggregation (CCA) to increase renewable energy use, lower costs, and support Sleepy Hollow's climate goals.
actions.funding: NYSERDA Community Choice Aggregation Toolkit, NYSERDA FlexTech
implementationProjects: Village Green Buildings & Parking Lots Upgrade Program

actions.action: Village Natural Area and Forestry Program
actions.description: Integrate natural systems into Sleepy Hollow's built environment through conservation, reforestation, and sustainable land management.
Transition to a circular economy by enhancing waste diversion, recycling, and composting systems in Sleepy Hollow.
actions.funding: NYSDEC Municipal Waste Reduction and Recycling Program (MWR&R), NYSAR Community Reuse Grant
implementationProjects: Organics and Food Scraps Management Program

actions.description: Expand access to locally produced food, reduce transportation emissions, and support sustainable agriculture in Sleepy Hollow.
actions.funding: NYSDAM Urban Farms and Community Gardens Grant, Cornell Cooperative Extension Urban Agriculture Program
implementationProjects: Village Green Buildings & Parking Lots Upgrade Program
Organics and Food Scraps Management Program

actions.description: Support local businesses in adopting sustainable practices to reduce their environmental impact and contribute to Sleepy Hollow's climate action goals.
actions.funding: SBEAP, NYSESD Small Business Environmental Support Office, SSBCI, NYSESD Linked Deposit Program, New York Green Bank
implementationProjects: Village Green Buildings & Parking Lots Upgrade Program

actions.action: Annual Village Climate Action Progress Reports
actions.description: Monitor, assess, and adapt Sleepy Hollow's Climate Action Plan (CAP) to ensure measurable progress toward sustainability goals.
actions.funding: NYSDEC CSC Grant and Technical Assistance Program

actions.description: Create a dedicated role to oversee Sleepy Hollow's climate initiatives, drive sustainability efforts, and secure funding for key projects.
actions.funding: NYSDEC CSC Grant and Technical Assistance Program

actions.description: Document and analyze where, when and why watershed systems fail during storm events.

actions.action: Conduct a Survey and Historic Analysis
actions.description: Identify existing and historic wetlands, headwaters, and underground streams for further assessment and intermunicipal planning.

actions.action: Design an Operations and Maintenance (O&M) Plan for Stormwater
actions.description: Ensure stormwater equipment and structures are renewed, operated and maintained with BMPs and updated regularly to account for climate change projections.

actions.action: Develop a Management Plan for Watershed and Tributaries
actions.description: Work with other municipalities and stakeholders along tributaries to develop a management plan addressing flooding causes in Sleepy Hollow.
Use native plants and natural elements in combination with engineered infrastructure to stabilize shorelines of the Hudson and Pocantico Rivers.
Integrate riverfront parks with coastal flood risk reduction. Repair bulkheads, address erosion, resize culverts, manage sediment, and create natural buffers.
Explore voluntary buyout program to protect homes and businesses from future flooding based on flood risk levels.

actions.action: Upgrade Stormwater Infrastructure with Green Infrastructure
actions.description: Integrate nature-based solutions and green infrastructure into any stormwater infrastructure upgrades.
Survey, evaluate, and strategically reengineer or replace combined sewer outfalls along the Hudson and Pocantico Rivers.
Upgrade aging 30-inch sewer main line parallel to Hudson River carrying wastewater to Yonkers treatment plant.
Develop a system for monitoring water quality and reporting it to the public, coordinated with adjacent river communities.
Inventory all sources of untreated runoff and develop a plan for filtering and redirecting them. Integrate green infrastructure into parking lots.
Maintain dam and infrastructure serving freshwater reservoirs. Address concerns about algal blooms and dam failures.
Plan and coordinate lake level drawdowns to offset flooding during storm conditions.
Improve water quality to support clean drinking water and healthy habitats for communities and ecosystems.

actions.action: Add More Hudson River Swimming, Boating, Fishing, and Kayaking
actions.description: Add more water-dependent recreational opportunities on the Hudson River. Prioritize in zoning codes and project review procedures.

actions.action: Prioritize Water-in/Water-out Businesses on the Hudson River
actions.description: Revitalize economic development on former industrial areas and transition from road to rivers ('Blue Highway'). Emergency planning for waterborne transportation.

actions.action: Prioritize a Network of Sponge Streetscapes
actions.description: Integrate green infrastructure along key street corridors — tree boxes, permeable paving, bioswales, and native plantings. Prioritize safe walking routes to schools.

actions.action: Unite around a Sponge Core (Sleepy Hollow Commons)
actions.description: Transform Sleepy Hollow Commons into the Sponge Core that restores watershed hydrology and wetland functions while providing central public open space.
Help residents adapt front and rear lawns into stormwater gardens that naturally slow, absorb, and recycle rainfall.
Identify existing and historic wetlands, headwaters, and underground streams. Many basement/yard flooding traced to former streams on historic maps.

actions.action: Manage Brooks and Study Daylighting Streams
actions.description: Evaluate streams for culvert sizes, road crossings, debris collection. Consider daylighting opportunities (e.g., Andre Brook through Reverend Sykes Park).
Collaborate with upstream towns (Ossining, Mount Pleasant, New Castle, Briarcliff Manor, Pleasantville) to manage inputs into the Pocantico River.
Upgrade and renew outdated stormwater and sewer infrastructure along with regular maintenance cycles.
Create community ownership through Adopt-a-Swale/Tree and Greenest Street programs. Encourage residents to maintain local green spaces.
Provide guidance on green infrastructure practices for homeowners including rain barrels, rain gardens, and sponge lawn techniques.
Pilot project on one side of a block in Philipse Manor with homeowner improvements, monitored against the other side over four seasons.
Set streetscape design benchmarks including expanding street tree canopy, shade structures, and reflective surface improvements for heat adaptation.
Create family-friendly cooling centers by reusing public buildings. Consider Galgano Senior Center as pilot. Tap Clean Green Schools program for funding.
Enhance natural shade and cooling through increased urban forestry. Trees intercept snow/rainwater and reduce runoff. Build on 150-tree anniversary planting.
Develop strategies to improve ecological resources, regulate development on hillsides and steep slopes, conserve existing forests from encroachment.
Update fire-management plans, emergency training and exercises. Develop east/west evacuation strategies for fires and floods. Address volunteer fire force response times.
Continue supporting Farm-to-Table and Buy Local/Buy Green movements. Support TASH outdoor market and Stone Barns Center partnerships.
Allocate resources to support community gardens and encourage shared commissary kitchens for small businesses.
Promote education around shifting to less resource-dependent plant-based diets. Reduce food waste to lower Village solid waste treatment costs.
Support food coops and food pantries to safeguard food security in Sleepy Hollow. Address interconnected chronic stressors such as inequity.

actions.action: Collaborate on Water Quality Improvements for Future Swimming and Fishing
actions.description: Long-term opportunity requiring water quality analysis during hotter seasons. Collaborate with upstream Villages to improve Pocantico River water quality.
